pr.title: Match/improve 52 functions across 16 TUs (1 at 100%, 35 at >=90%)
pr.author: davidfeira

summary: Large decomp-matching batch adding source for 52 previously stubbed functions across fighter, game-mode, ground/stage, trophy/toy, library, and sysdolphin TUs. The PR reported 1 exact function match, many high-percent improvements, strict CI success with --max-errors 0 --require-protos --map, and documented regression tradeoffs caused by data/literal-pool movement when new function bodies were compiled into existing TUs.

key_files.role: Added 7 Kirby copy-ability special function bodies and include dependencies; excerpt shows loops over costume/joint/DObj data, switch over FighterKind, PAD_STACK, and scoped pragma dont_inline.
Added 4 Kirby/Yoshi special function bodies and include dependency for ityoshiegglay.
Added 2 ftcpuattack function bodies; later comment cites small sdata2/literal reorder regressions for this TU.
Added gm_80164910 body at 98.81%.
Added 4 camera function bodies and contributed a .sdata exact-match report item.
Added 3 stage function bodies including fn_801F3F74 at 100%; comment later cites this TU as affected by small literal-pool reorder artifacts.
Added 2 grKongo bodies; issue comment explains sibling functions regressed slightly due to new constants in grkongo.o sdata2.
Added 2 grmaterial function bodies.
Added 3 ground entries per PR body, including one listed as void at 0.00%, indicating weak function identity evidence for that entry.
Small file churn with many hunks; issue comment attributes 10 tiny regressions to the single added body grIceMt_801F7D94 and sdata2/literal-pool reorder effects.
Added grPura_80212FC0 and aligned grPura_80213030 signature to Ground_GObj* according to PR notes.
Largest changed file by added lines; added 4 grVenom bodies and was cited among TUs with expected tiny reorder regressions.
Added lbArq_80014BD0 body at 93.97%; report also listed lbarq .sdata exact-match improvement.

PR body lists 5 toy.c function additions, but later author comment says toy.c was reverted to upstream entirely to fix data-section layout regression; changed_files metadata in this slice does not list toy.c, only toy.h.
Added 6 tylist function bodies; one of the largest files by added lines.
Added 6 baselib/card-state related function bodies.
Added HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress body at 96.56%.
Updated fn_800DA8E4 prototype from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void with Fighter_GObj*, Fighter_GObj*, s32.
Updated ftKb_SpecialN_800EF438 return type from UNK_RET to void.
Added prototypes for un_80312BAC, un_8031305C, fn_80313BD8, and fn_8031438C.
PR note says un_8030813C arg type stays s32 because it matches toy.c body.
PR note says gm_8016A9E8 declaration was updated from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to int(u8, s8), but changed_files in the loaded slice lists gm_1601.c rather than gm_1601.h; path requires curator caution.
Header/type changes supporting recovered bodies.
Fighter type changes supporting recovered bodies.
Header updates supporting ftcpuattack recovered bodies.
Static header updates supporting gmcamera recovered bodies.
Static header updates supporting grkongo recovered bodies.
Ground type/header change supporting recovered ground/stage bodies.
Header update supporting lbArq_80014BD0 body.
Header updates supporting recovered hsd_3AA7 bodies.

what_changed: Replaced many placeholder markers with C source bodies across 16 translation units, with the PR body listing 52 functions and match/improvement percentages.
Updated several headers from unknown or stub signatures to more specific prototypes, including fn_800DA8E4 and ftKb_SpecialN_800EF438 in the diff excerpt.
Added include dependencies surfaced by strict prototype checking, including examples listed in the PR body such as lb/lbvector.h, ft/ftlib.h, gr/grmaterial.h, baselib/debug.h, and baselib/id.h.
Reverted earlier ftCo_0A01.c additions after they degraded existing upstream matches; PR body says those functions need a separate approach.
Reverted divergent toy.c changes after a broken un_80309338 match was traced to data-section layout movement; the loaded changed-files list no longer includes toy.c.
Accepted or documented small remaining unmatched-tier regressions attributed by the author to sdata2/literal-pool reorder artifacts from adding new function bodies.

smart_moves.text: The PR author removed ftCo_0A01.c additions when they caused severe existing-match regressions, preserving upstream matches instead of landing broad source churn.
After review/report feedback, the author reverted toy.c to upstream to fix un_80309338 and clear seven toy.c neighbor regressions caused by non-body divergence.
The PR documents the tradeoff for remaining tiny regressions: new function bodies emit literals into TU-local sections, changing load displacement bytes in unchanged sibling functions.
The PR used strict CI flags with require-protos and map output, then added includes/prototypes surfaced by those checks.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Large batches of new function bodies can perturb existing near/exact matches through TU-local data-section and literal-pool movement, even when sibling function source bodies are unchanged.
When non-body churn such as struct redefs, include reorder, or new externs causes data-layout movement, reverting that churn can restore broken matches without discarding all decomp progress.
Below-80% structurally plausible bodies may be useful as permuter starting points, but the PR itself frames their acceptability as review-dependent rather than as completed matches.
Recovered function bodies should be accompanied by owning header prototype updates and include fixes when strict prototype checking surfaces missing declarations.

naming_conventions.convention: The PR keeps many address-style names such as fn_801F3F74, fn_80313BD8, and hsd_803B2374 where semantic names are not evidenced.
Local variable names in the diff use established short role names where obvious, such as fp, gobj, jobj, dobj, mobj, hat, and bone.
The diff uses subsystem-specific enum constants for FighterKind switch cases, e.g. FTKIND_MARIO, FTKIND_LUIGI, FTKIND_YOSHI, rather than raw fighter-kind numbers.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Scoped pragma dont_inline was used around ftKb_SpecialN_800F11F0 with #pragma push/#pragma pop.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Loaded evidence does not include the specific objdiff justification for this pragma; current standards require narrow evidence before retaining pragma tactics.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK(8) appears in recovered Kirby functions, including ftKb_SpecialN_800EF0E4 and ftKb_SpecialN_800F11F0.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Loaded evidence shows use but not the exact verification reason; treat as a matching tactic needing objdiff evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: For dispatch over many fighter kinds, the recovered source uses a switch with named FTKIND_* cases.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: This aligns with canonical control-flow standards, but the PR excerpt does not show alternative attempts.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: The author triaged regressions by distinguishing unchanged function-body diffs from changed displacement operands caused by local data-section movement.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: This is author-provided explanation plus bot report context, not independently reproduced by this intake.

review_feedback.feedback: There were no loaded inline review comments or formal reviews, but the author response says 'Thanks for the review' and reports pushing commit 85d5a32.
A bot report showed new matches/improvements and regressions; the author fixed the broken un_80309338 regression by reverting toy.c divergence.
The author offered to revert remaining TU additions if a cleaner regression report was preferred, but loaded evidence does not include reviewer intent or a reply choosing that tradeoff.

follow_up_queries: Find future PRs mentioning sdata2-relative encodings or literal-pool reorder artifacts after adding source bodies.
Search for ftCo_0A01 follow-up PRs that reintroduce the reverted 16 function bodies with permuter-friendly variants or NON_MATCHING gating.
Search for PRs touching ftKb_SpecialN_800F11F0 to see whether #pragma dont_inline or PAD_STACK was later removed or justified.
Search for later cleanup of raw FighterBone pointer arithmetic and gw union-arm fields in ftKb_Init.c.
Search for verify_pr.py references in later PRs, since this PR says it was added locally but not necessarily committed.

notes: PR identity, state, changed-file list, function lists, and author explanations are well represented.
The full raw diff is truncated in loaded context; only ftKb_Init and nearby header/include excerpts are visible in detail.
No inline review comments or formal reviews are loaded; review feedback is inferred only from the bot report and the author's issue comment, without assigning reviewer intent.

The PR body lists toy.c function additions, but changed_files metadata in the final slice does not include toy.c and the author says toy.c was reverted entirely; final-state toy.c claims should be treated cautiously.
The PR note mentions src/melee/gm/gm_1601.h, but changed_files metadata lists src/melee/gm/gm_1601.c and not gm_1601.h; path-level evidence is inconsistent for that header note.
Several match percentages are PR-authored or bot-reported; this intake did not run builds, objdiff, or verification tools.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Same-TU source additions can cause tiny sdata2/literal-pool regressions in unchanged siblings
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2469 documents that compiling newly recovered function bodies can emit string/float literals into TU-local sections, changing 16-bit displacement operands in unchanged sibling functions and producing small byte-level regressions. The author cited grkongo.c, gricemt.c, grvenom.c, grpura.c, grfourside.c, and ftcpuattack.c examples.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Revert unrelated source/data-layout churn when it breaks an existing match
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2469, toy.c divergence beyond function-body additions shifted toy.o data layout and broke un_80309338 despite an unchanged body. Reverting toy.c to upstream fixed that broken match and cleared seven nearby toy.c regressions.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Low-percent bodies should be framed as starting points unless verifier evidence supports stronger claims
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2469 explicitly labels several below-80% entries as structurally correct but regalloc/scheduling-blocked or partial, useful as permuter starting points and acceptable to reject. This is evidence for cautious claim language around low-score decomp submissions.

source_update_candidates.title: Mention same-TU literal/data movement in regression-ledger guidance
source_update_candidates.text: When adding source bodies to a TU, check for sibling regressions caused by new local strings/floats changing .sdata2 or literal-pool displacement operands. If bodies are unchanged and only load offsets differ, record that narrow evidence and decide whether the added matches justify the report noise.
source_update_candidates.reason: PR #2469 provides concrete regression-triage evidence that section-local literal movement can create small sibling diffs after adding source bodies.

source_update_candidates.title: Clarify review posture for below-80% decomp bodies
source_update_candidates.text: For low-percent recovered bodies submitted as structurally correct starting points, require explicit labeling as partial/permuter-ready and avoid treating them as accepted matches unless local objdiff evidence supports that claim.
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR body distinguishes high-confidence matched/improved bodies from below-80% partial starting points and says rejecting them is fine.

source_update_candidates.title: ftCo_0A01.c large body additions were reverted due to regression blast radius
source_update_candidates.text: An earlier revision of PR #2469 added 16 ftCo_0A01.c bodies, but the author reverted them because file-layout changes broke 3 upstream 100% matches and severely degraded 4 near-matches. The author suggested a future separate PR using a different approach such as permuter-friendly variants or NON_MATCHING gating.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a path-specific caution from the PR body; curator should decide whether it belongs in path facts.

source_update_candidates.title: toy.c data-layout sensitivity after non-body divergence
source_update_candidates.text: In PR #2469, toy.c divergence from upstream by 177 lines beyond function-body additions shifted toy.o data by about 1.3KB and broke un_80309338 through sdata2-relative encodings. Reverting toy.c to upstream fixed the match and neighboring regressions.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is concrete path-specific regression evidence, but final changed-files do not include toy.c, so curator review is needed before storing as a path fact.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the PR's low-percent bodies as accepted matches; the author frames several as partial/permuter starting points.
Do not infer reviewer intent from 'Thanks for the review'; no review comments or formal reviews are loaded.
Do not claim verify_pr.py was committed in this PR; the author says it was added locally.
Do not treat the toy.c function list as final changed-file evidence without curator verification, because final changed_files omits toy.c and the author says it was reverted.
